category_title: Oppositional and boisterous students with disruptive behaviors
minimal_context: Edward is standing behind Trey at the computer during morning menu to collaborate on a project.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Edward, it is time for morning menu. Please work on the computer project with Trey.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Standing behind Trey) The Astronomy Picture of the Day. Saturn’s moon has (inaudible) on it. Whoops! Comet over California. Whoosh!

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: Alien monster gods. Go back there, Trey. I found something that’s like radar, and it’s so cool . . .

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: You need to compromise with your partner.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Loudly) No! I'm not compromising! Trey is doing it wrong! Move!

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: Edward, it is time for morning menu. Please work on the computer project with Trey.

STUDENT: (Standing behind Trey) The Astronomy Picture of the Day. Saturn’s moon has (inaudible) on it. Whoops! Comet over California. Whoosh!

TUTOR: Remember to share the computer.

STUDENT: Alien monster gods. Go back there, Trey. I found something that’s like radar, and it’s so cool . . .

TUTOR: You need to compromise with your partner.

STUDENT: (Loudly) No! I'm not compromising! Trey is doing it wrong! Move!

task.content: Working on projects with other students and using the computer

context.social_setting: Collaborating with peers; standing behind another student working on the computer

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: When attempting to collaborate on projects or share materials with peers
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: Exhibits overbearing behavior, argues, shows unwillingness to compromise, and relentlessly teases other students (especially girls), sometimes bringing them to tears.
related_quotes: The Astronomy Picture of the Day. Saturn’s moon has (inaudible) on it. Whoops! Comet over California. Whoosh! ... Alien monster gods. Go back there, Trey. I found something that’s like radar, and it’s so cool . . .

behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: Attempts dissolve into arguments over his oppositional behavior and unwillingness to compromise; other students refuse to work with him.
